description:
## What
Implement local-only bookmarks. Bookmarks are stored locally and never federated. POST /api/v1/statuses/:id/bookmark adds a bookmark. POST /api/v1/statuses/:id/unbookmark removes it. GET /api/v1/bookmarks returns a paginated list of bookmarked statuses as Status objects. Requires a bookmarks table keyed on (account_id, post_id or remote_post_id).

## Why
Bookmarks are used heavily in Ivory and Elk as a reading list. Without them the bookmark button appears to do nothing. Part of sub-epic 8-D.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social GET /api/v1/bookmarks to document the Status array response shape and Link header pagination format; check Status object for the bookmarked boolean field and document when it is true; verify POST /api/v1/statuses/:id/bookmark returns the full updated Status object (not 204).
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add bookmarks migration (id snowflake PRIMARY KEY, account_id INTEGER, post_id INTEGER NULLABLE, remote_post_id INTEGER NULLABLE, created_at INTEGER, UNIQUE(account_id, post_id), UNIQUE(account_id, remote_post_id), CHECK(post_id IS NOT NULL OR remote_post_id IS NOT NULL)); implement POST /api/v1/statuses/:id/bookmark: resolve status ID to local or remote post; INSERT OR IGNORE into bookmarks; return full Status object with bookmarked=true; implement POST /api/v1/statuses/:id/unbookmark: DELETE from bookmarks; return full Status object with bookmarked=false; implement GET /api/v1/bookmarks: paginated query with max_id/min_id/since_id/limit support using snowflake ordering on bookmarks.id; JOIN to posts/remote_posts; serialize each as Status; set Link header.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests with in-memory SQLite; create local post; bookmark it; assert GET /api/v1/bookmarks returns it with bookmarked=true; unbookmark it; assert it does not appear in bookmarks list; assert Status.bookmarked=false in other endpoints after unbookmark; assert bookmark is per-account (account A's bookmark not visible to account B).

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social GET /api/v1/bookmarks live fixture for Status array and Link header format; mastodon.social POST /api/v1/statuses/:id/bookmark response shape (returns full Status, not 204).
- Test approach: integration test with in-memory SQLite; create two local personas; bookmark post with persona A; assert it appears in persona A's bookmarks; assert it does NOT appear in persona B's bookmarks (isolation check); pagination test: create 5 bookmarks; query with limit=2; follow Link header next; verify non-overlapping pages.

## Defensive programming checklist
- Bookmark is per-persona not per-server (always filter by authed account_id)
- Double-bookmark is idempotent (INSERT OR IGNORE); return 200 not 422
- Double-unbookmark is idempotent; return 200 with Status.bookmarked=false
- Bookmarking a nonexistent status returns 404 (not panic)
- No .unwrap() in handler paths
- Pagination uses snowflake IDs on bookmarks.id, not OFFSET
title: GET/POST/DELETE /api/v1/bookmarks — local per-persona bookmark table
